subject: Breast
title: Bloody Nipple Discharge Decoded
hook: One benign lesion explains most bloody nipple discharge.

points.heading: The bloody discharge culprit
points.icon: a duct with a small papilloma
bullets: Solitary papilloma is the top pathological cause
Arises within a single major subareolar duct

points.heading: How you fix it
bullets: Microdochectomy removes just the affected duct
Total duct excision is used in older women
Surgery confirms diagnosis and stops the discharge

points.heading: When there are many
points.icon: five small papillomas inside a duct
bullets: Five or more papillomas define papilloma syndrome
This alone raises cancer risk above baseline
Atypia present raises that risk even further

points.heading: Exam traps
bullets: Pregnancy discharge from hypervascularity is usually benign
Nipple adenoma also bleeds and mimics Paget's disease
Punch biopsy separates adenoma from true Paget's

cta: Do not miss the bleeding duct
captionLines: Bloody nipple discharge has one classic culprit. 🔴
Solitary intraductal papilloma is the most common pathological cause in women aged 30 to 50.
Multiple papillomas raise cancer risk on their own. Atypia raises it further.
Recall Engine turns any lecture into SBAs, MCQs, flashcards and high-yield notes so nipple discharge questions stop catching you out.
